findings.title: Cosine similarity definition missing ‖z‖ in denominator
findings.snippet: Line 191 (Definition 1): ρ(y, z) = (y · z) / ‖y‖.  Line 490 (Proof of Theorem 1): ρ(y*, u^ℓ(G)) = (y* · u^ℓ(G)) / (‖y*‖ ‖u^ℓ(G)‖).
findings.explanation: Definition 1 gives the cosine similarity as ρ(y,z) = (y·z)/‖y‖, but the standard cosine similarity—and the formula actually used in the proof of Theorem 1 at line 490—is ρ(y,z) = (y·z)/(‖y‖‖z‖). The denominator is missing ‖z‖. Under the stated definition, ρ(y,z)=1 when z is a positive scaling of y would yield ρ = c‖y‖ ≠ 1, contradicting the text's own interpretation. The error is invisible in later results only because the second argument is always a unit eigenvector u^ℓ with ‖u^ℓ‖=1.
findings.fix: Change the definition to ρ(y, z) = (y · z) / (‖y‖ ‖z‖).

findings.title: Assumption 3 missing squared norm
findings.snippet: Line 198: 'when the budget is large enough—that is, C ≥ ‖b̂‖²'.  Line 200 (Assumption 3): 'Either w < 0 and C < ‖b̂‖, or w > 0.'
findings.explanation: The paragraph preceding Assumption 3 correctly notes the planner can achieve the first-best when C ≥ ‖b̂‖². Assumption 3 is meant to rule out exactly that case, so it should state C < ‖b̂‖², but it writes C < ‖b̂‖ (missing the exponent 2). The cost function is K = Σ(b_i − b̂_i)² ≤ C, so the relevant threshold is ‖b̂‖², not ‖b̂‖.
findings.fix: Change Assumption 3 to read: 'Either w < 0 and C < ‖b̂‖², or w > 0.'

findings.title: Eigenvectors u^n, u^{n-1} called 'maximizers' of minimization problems
findings.snippet: Lines 321–322 define λ_n = min_{u:‖u‖=1} Σ g_{ij} u_i u_j and λ_{n-1} = min_{u:‖u‖=1, u·u^n=0} Σ g_{ij} u_i u_j.  Line 324: 'Moreover, the eigenvector u^n is a maximizer of the first problem, while u^{n-1} is a maximizer of the second.'
findings.explanation: The variational problems for the bottom eigenvalues are stated as minimizations (lines 321–322), so the solutions u^n and u^{n-1} are minimizers, not maximizers. Compare with lines 312–316, where the top eigenvalues are correctly stated as maximization problems and u^1, u^2 are correctly called maximizers. The later sentence on the same page ('u^n = argmin …') uses the correct term, confirming the word 'maximizer' earlier is an error.
findings.fix: Change 'maximizer' to 'minimizer' in both instances on line 324: 'Moreover, the eigenvector u^n is a minimizer of the first problem, while u^{n-1} is a minimizer of the second.'

findings.title: Lagrangian in proof of Theorem 1 has b̂_ℓ instead of b̂_ℓ²
findings.snippet: Line 465 (Lagrangian): L = w Σ α_ℓ (1+x_ℓ)² b̂_ℓ + μ[C − Σ b̂_ℓ² x_ℓ²].  Line 451 (Objective IT-PC): max_x  w Σ α_ℓ (1+x_ℓ)² b̂_ℓ².
findings.explanation: The objective function of problem (IT-PC) at line 451 contains the factor b̂_ℓ² (squared), but the Lagrangian at line 465 writes the first sum with only b̂_ℓ (no square). The correctly derived FOC at line 471, which factors out 2b̂_ℓ², is consistent with the squared version, confirming that the Lagrangian's first term is missing the exponent.
findings.fix: In line 465, replace b̂_ℓ with b̂_ℓ² in the first summation, so the Lagrangian reads: L = w Σ α_ℓ (1+x_ℓ)² b̂_ℓ² + μ[C − Σ b̂_ℓ² x_ℓ²].

findings.title: Missing tilde in Example 2 change of variables
findings.snippet: Line 128: 'Performing the change of variables b_i = [τ − b_i]/2 and β = −β̃/2 (with the status quo equal to b̂_i = [τ − b̃_i]/2).'
findings.explanation: The change-of-variables formula writes b_i = [τ − b_i]/2, which is self-referential (b_i on both sides, yielding b_i = τ/3). The right-hand side should use the tilde'd original parameter: b_i = [τ − b̃_i]/2. The correct form is confirmed by the status quo expression on the same line, b̂_i = [τ − b̃_i]/2, which correctly uses b̃_i, and by the FOC derivation of Example 2.

findings.title: Discussion paragraph swaps denominator sign relative to Proposition 2
findings.snippet: Line 296 (Proposition 2, part 2): (α_{n-1}/(α_n − α_{n-1}))².  Line 301 (discussion): 'the term α_{n-1}/(α_{n-1} − α_n) is large when …'
findings.explanation: Proposition 2 part 2 uses the ratio α_{n-1}/(α_n − α_{n-1}), which is positive because α_n > α_{n-1} when β < 0. The discussion paragraph reverses the denominator to α_{n-1}/(α_{n-1} − α_n), which is negative (since α_{n-1} − α_n < 0). The text then says this quantity 'is large,' which is incoherent for a negative quantity. The denominator signs are swapped between the proposition and the discussion.
findings.fix: Change the discussion to read: 'the term α_{n-1}/(α_n − α_{n-1}) is large when the difference λ_{n-1} − λ_n … is small.'

findings.title: Swapped sub/superscripts on eigenvector entry in proof of Proposition 2
findings.snippet: Line 584 (Proof of Prop 2): b_i* − b̂_i = w Σ_ℓ u_ℓ^i (α_ℓ/(μ − wα_ℓ)) b̂_ℓ.  Line 186 (convention): a_i* = Σ_ℓ (1/(1−βλ_ℓ)) u_i^ℓ b_ℓ.
findings.explanation: The paper's convention (established at line 142 and used at line 186) is u_i^ℓ, where the subscript i denotes the node and the superscript ℓ denotes the eigenvector index. Line 584 writes u_ℓ^i, swapping subscript and superscript. While these entries of an orthogonal matrix are numerically equal (u_i^ℓ = U_{iℓ} = (U^⊤)_{ℓi}), the notation is inconsistent with the rest of the paper and could confuse readers.
findings.fix: Change u_ℓ^i to u_i^ℓ on line 584 to match the paper's established convention.

findings.title: Prime placed on wrong symbol in proof of Proposition 1
findings.snippet: Line 498: r_ℓ*/r_{ℓ'}* = (α_ℓ/α_{ℓ'}) · (μ − wα_ℓ')/(μ − wα_ℓ), where the second fraction's numerator uses α_ℓ' (prime as superscript on α_ℓ) instead of α_{ℓ'} (α subscripted by ℓ').
findings.explanation: The denominator of the first fraction correctly writes α_{ℓ'} (subscript ℓ'), but the numerator of the second fraction writes α_{ℓ}^{′} (α subscripted by ℓ with a separate prime mark), which in LaTeX renders differently. This is a typesetting error: the prime should be part of the subscript (ℓ'), not a separate superscript on α.
findings.fix: Change α_{ℓ}^{′} to α_{ℓ'} in the numerator of the second fraction.

findings.title: Misplaced asterisk on transposed vector in equation (9)
findings.explanation: In the second step of the chain of equalities, the expression (a̲⊤)* places the equilibrium asterisk on the transposition rather than on the vector. It should read (a̲*)⊤(a̲*) to be consistent with the first step (a*)⊤a*. The superscript * denoting equilibrium ended up attached to the transpose operator rather than to the vector a̲.
